Paths/Full Stack Development/Full Stack Overview

Full Stack Overview

Understand how frontend and backend work together in a modern web application.

Full stack development means working across the entire web application — from the UI to the server to the database.

Key Concepts

  • Client-Server Model: The browser (client) makes requests; the server responds with data or HTML.
  • API Layer: The bridge between frontend and backend, typically REST or GraphQL.
  • Frontend: What the user sees — HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React/Next.js.
  • Backend: Server logic, authentication, database operations.
  • DevOps: Deployment, CI/CD, monitoring, and infrastructure.

The Modern Stack

User → Browser → Next.js (Frontend)
                    ↓
              API Routes (Backend)
                    ↓
              Database (MongoDB/PostgreSQL)
